body:
## Summary
- allow `delegate_issue` with `child_issue_number` to adopt an existing user-authored issue instead of requiring the child issue itself to be agent-created
- store adopted child state in an agent-authored child issue comment, while keeping parent child-link and terminal report markers agent-authored/trusted
- resolve and update terminal child state from either trusted issue-body markers or trusted child-comment markers
- update orchestrator docs/prompt to describe create/reuse/adopt behavior

This unblocks the #83 case where the parent issue links existing issues (#84, #79) and asks the orchestrator to work through them one at a time.

## Validation
- `npm --prefix .agent test` (440 tests passed)
- `npm --prefix .agent run build`

manual orchestrate start on PR; dispatching review

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777742799893 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUyNTc1ODA3NDA6b3JjaGVzdHJhdGU6ODU6cmV2aWV3Ojg1OjI -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 98 | PASS | 9 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR body and implementation target the linked #83 case with #84/#79 child issues; docs and prompt changes reflect the broader orchestrator behavior. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | Keeps `child_issue_number` as the structured planner field and preserves validation via `parseOptionalChildIssueNumber`; no free-form command parsing added. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Reuses `parseSubOrchestratorMarker`, marker formatting/update helpers, existing issue/comment fetch/update helpers, and child-link markers instead of new marker logic. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updates `.agent/docs/actions/agent-actions.md`, `.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md`, `.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md`, and `.github/prompts/agent-orchestrator.md`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Comment on accepted workflow stops | pass | 6/6 | Existing delegation failure stop comment path remains; adoption now writes an agent-authored child comment and parent child-link comment for visible state. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | Missing marker comment ids throw, comment/body updates use trusted marker source, and delegation failures are surfaced through stop reason/comment paths. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | Adoption trusts only authenticated agent/app-authored body or comment markers; user-authored issue bodies are not trusted for child state. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Diff is scoped to orchestrator handoff code, focused tests, and matching docs/prompt updates. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 6/7 | The implementation is straightforward and localized, though the trusted marker record plumbing adds some necessary complexity for body-vs-comment state. |

## Notes

- The strongest rubric drivers are the trust boundary and documentation coverage: adopted user-authored issues store child state only in agent-authored comments, while parent links and terminal reports remain agent-authored.
- Regression coverage includes explicit adoption of a user-authored issue with a forged body marker and terminal reporting from an agent-authored adoption marker comment.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific blocking or warning findings. The PR satisfies the applicable selected rubrics.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #85 lets `delegate_issue` adopt existing user-authored issues as child orchestrator targets, which unblocks #83’s request to process linked issues #84 and #79 one by one.
- The design keeps the trust boundary intact by storing adopted child state in an agent-authored issue comment instead of editing or trusting the user-authored child issue body.
- It also updates terminal reporting so trusted sub-orchestrator markers can be resolved from either issue bodies or trusted comments.
- Docs and planner prompt text are updated to describe create, reuse, and adopt behavior.

## Review
- Overall, the trust model and documentation changes are sound, but one runtime validation gap needs a branch fix before this should merge.
- Reviewers disagreed on final severity: one reviewer considered the PR shippable with low-risk test gaps, while another identified a blocking issue around adopting invalid GitHub issue-shaped targets.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Explicit adoption can target PRs or closed issues | BLOCKING | The adoption branch accepts the `gh issue view` result without verifying the target is an open issue, so a PR number or closed issue can be adopted and dispatched as `target_kind: "issue"`. |
| Already-adopted reruns lack direct regression coverage | WARNING | Sequential reruns should skip creating a second adoption comment when a trusted adoption marker already exists, but that idempotency path is not directly tested. |
| Rubrics and documentation are aligned | INFO | The rubrics review passed, and the changed docs/prompt accurately describe the new adopt behavior and trusted marker storage model. |

## Progress
- The PR already fixes the #83 blocker by allowing user-authored child issues to be adopted via trusted agent-authored comments.
- Tests cover adoption of a user-authored issue with a forged body marker and terminal reporting from an agent-authored adoption marker comment.
- Documentation was updated across the relevant orchestrator action, architecture, technical details, and planner prompt files.
- A new inline comment was posted on [`orchestrate-handoff.ts` line 522](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/781774a66afb0ccda91f47fde264d46e0a58978a/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L522) for the blocking validation issue.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Explicit adoption can target PRs or closed issues</summary>

**Cause:** [`ensureSubOrchestrationIssue`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/781774a66afb0ccda91f47fde264d46e0a58978a/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L518) fetches the supplied `child_issue_number` and adopts it when no trusted marker exists, but the fetched record does not prove the target is an open issue rather than a PR or closed issue.

**Candidate solutions:** Fetch enough metadata to reject pull requests and non-open issues before `adoptExistingSubOrchestrationIssue`, then add regressions for PR-number rejection and closed-issue rejection.

**Comments:** This is the main reviewer disagreement: the trust-marker design is good, but the widened adoption surface needs an explicit target-type/state guard.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Already-adopted reruns lack direct regression coverage</summary>

**Cause:** The new code appears idempotent by resolving trusted adoption comments before adopting, but no focused test proves a rerun with an existing trusted adoption comment avoids a duplicate child comment.

**Candidate solutions:** Add a regression where `FAKE_ISSUE_COMMENTS_JSON` contains an existing trusted adoption marker for the child issue and assert the run dispatches without POSTing a new child adoption comment.

**Comments:** This is lower risk than the target-validation gap because the current implementation is safe by structure, but coverage would protect the intended rerun behavior.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: the unresolved validation gap requires a concrete branch change and is safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
NEEDS_REWORK

## Action Items
- [ ] Validate that explicit `child_issue_number` targets are open issues, not PRs or closed issues, before adoption/reuse dispatch.
- [ ] Add regression coverage for rejecting PR-number and closed-issue adoption targets.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 3/10).

agent planner selected fix-pr: Latest review synthesis recommends FIX_PR for a blocking validation gap in explicit child issue adoption.

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `fix/adopt-existing-delegate-issues`. Requested by @sepo-agent.

- Fetched `state`/`url` for explicit `child_issue_number` targets and reject PR URLs or non-OPEN issue states before trusted reuse/adoption.
- Added orchestrator handoff regressions for PR-number and closed-issue child targets, asserting no adoption comment or child dispatch occurs.
- Validation: `npm --prefix .agent test` (442 tests passed).

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `fix-pr` -> `review` (round 4/10).

agent planner selected review: fix-pr succeeded after addressing the blocking adoption-target validation findings; one review pass should verify the branch changes before stopping.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777743642697 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUyNTc3NzkxOTI6Zml4X3ByOjg1OnJldmlldzo4NTo0 -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 99 | PASS | 10 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR is aligned with #83, which links user-authored #84 and #79; the implementation directly removes the prior stop reason that #84 “was not created by the authenticated agent.” |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | Keeps `child_issue_number` as the explicit planner field and validates it through the existing structured path before adoption. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Reuses existing sub-orchestrator marker parsing/formatting and trusted actor checks, adding source-aware marker plumbing around them instead of a separate marker system. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updates orchestrator docs and prompt text to describe create/reuse/adopt behavior and trusted child-comment markers. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Comment on accepted workflow stops | pass | 6/6 | Delegation failures still become visible stop comments, and new adoption state is written visibly in agent-authored child/parent comments. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | Missing trusted marker comments throw, invalid explicit child targets stop visibly, and tests cover PR-number and closed-issue rejection. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | User-authored issue bodies are not trusted for child state; adopted state is stored only in trusted agent-authored comments, and explicit targets reject PRs/closed issues. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Diff is scoped to `.agent` orchestrator handoff logic, focused tests, and matching docs/prompt updates. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | Documentation changes describe behavior and state ownership without adding file inventory tables. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 6/7 | The approach is localized and direct; the only added complexity is the necessary body-vs-comment trusted marker record. |

## Notes

- The strongest score drivers are the trust boundary and follow-up fix: the PR now adopts user-authored issues without trusting user-authored markers, and it rejects PR-number or closed-issue `child_issue_number` targets before dispatch.
- Tests cover adoption with a forged body marker, terminal reporting from an agent-authored adoption marker comment, and explicit rejection of PR and closed issue targets.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific blocking or warning findings. The implementation satisfies the applicable selected rubrics.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #85 lets `delegate_issue` adopt existing user-authored issues as sub-orchestrator children, unblocking #83’s request to work through #84 and #79 one at a time.
- The adopted child state is stored in an agent-authored child issue comment, preserving the trust boundary instead of editing or trusting user-authored issue bodies.
- Terminal reporting now resolves and updates trusted child markers from either issue bodies or trusted comments.
- The follow-up fix already added validation so explicit child targets cannot be PRs or closed issues.

## Review
- Overall, the implementation is sound and the previous blocking validation issue appears addressed. The remaining concerns are minor hardening and regression coverage gaps.
- Reviewers agreed the trust model is appropriate; the only meaningful disagreement is final strictness, with one reviewer calling this shippable and another preferring the remaining rerun coverage before merge.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Asymmetric validation for fresh adoptions | WARNING | Fresh adoptions skip `validateReusableChildIssue()` while reused trusted children run it, leaving the invariant implicit instead of enforced after both branches. |
| Already-adopted reruns lack direct regression coverage | WARNING | The code appears idempotent, but no test proves an already-adopted child issue avoids a second adoption comment on rerun. |
| Rubrics and docs are aligned | INFO | The rubrics review passed, and the docs/prompt updates accurately describe create, reuse, and adopt behavior. |

## Progress
- The prior blocking issue around adopting PR numbers or closed issues was fixed with explicit target validation and regressions.
- Tests now cover user-authored adoption with a forged body marker, PR-number rejection, closed-issue rejection, and terminal reporting from trusted adoption comments.
- Documentation and planner prompt text were updated for the new adoption behavior.
- I posted one new inline comment on `.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts` line 549 for the validation-invariant concern.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Asymmetric validation for fresh adoptions</summary>

**Cause:** `ensureSubOrchestrationIssue()` validates only when `trustedIssue` already exists. Fresh adoption is currently safe because `adoptExistingSubOrchestrationIssue()` constructs the marker from the requested parent and stage, but that invariant is not checked after adoption.

**Candidate solutions:** Resolve `childIssue` first, then call `validateReusableChildIssue(childIssue, parentIssue, stage)` unconditionally before updating parent round and recording the child.

**Comments:** This is defensive hardening, not an observed runtime bug.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Already-adopted reruns lack direct regression coverage</summary>

**Cause:** The implementation should skip adoption when `trustedSubOrchestrationIssue()` finds an existing trusted child marker comment, but no focused test asserts that no second child adoption comment is posted.

**Candidate solutions:** Add a rerun test with `FAKE_ISSUE_COMMENTS_JSON` containing an existing trusted marker for the child issue, then assert dispatch occurs without POSTing another `issues/77/comments` adoption marker.

**Comments:** This was already covered in the prior synthesis, so I did not duplicate it with another inline comment.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: the remaining warnings are concrete, low-risk branch changes that an automated fix-pr pass can handle.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Make reusable-child validation unconditional after explicit child reuse/adoption resolves `childIssue`.
- [ ] Add a rerun/idempotency regression for already-adopted child issues.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 5/10).

agent planner selected fix-pr: Latest review synthesis recommends FIX_PR for concrete MINOR_ISSUES that are safe to automate in round 4/10.

@sepo-agent /answer doesn't reviewer say these are not fixes? I'm confused

No. The reviewer is **not** saying the earlier changes “are not fixes.”

There are two review rounds:

- The first blocking review said: reject `child_issue_number` targets that are PRs or closed issues. That was fixed.
- The later review says those fixes are present, but there are **two remaining warning-level follow-ups**:
  - Make `validateReusableChildIssue(childIssue, parentIssue, stage)` run unconditionally after either reuse or adoption resolves `childIssue`: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/85#discussion_r3176999184
  - Add a rerun/idempotency regression proving an already-adopted child issue does not get a second adoption marker comment.

So the “no working tree changes were needed” response was misleading. It re-checked the **previous blocking fix**, but it missed the **newer MINOR_ISSUES action items** from the latest review synthesis.

If you want to continue automation, the requested fixes should be restated as:

> Please address the latest MINOR_ISSUES review: make reusable-child validation unconditional after explicit child reuse/adoption resolves `childIssue`, and add a focused rerun test where an existing trusted adoption marker comment is reused without posting another child adoption comment.

> No. The reviewer is **not** saying the earlier changes “are not fixes.”
> 
> There are two review rounds:
> 
> - The first blocking review said: reject `child_issue_number` targets that are PRs or closed issues. That was fixed.
> - The later review says those fixes are present, but there are **two remaining warning-level follow-ups**:

>   - Add a rerun/idempotency regression proving an already-adopted child issue does not get a second adoption marker comment.

@sepo-agent /fix-pr I think the minor issues are reasonable to fix?

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `fix/adopt-existing-delegate-issues`. Requested by @lolipopshock.

Implemented the two minor follow-ups from the latest review.

- Made `validateReusableChildIssue(childIssue, parentIssue, stage)` run unconditionally after explicit `child_issue_number` resolves to either a trusted existing marker or a fresh adoption marker.
- Added a focused rerun regression proving an already-adopted user-authored issue with a trusted agent-authored marker comment is reused without posting another adoption marker comment.
- Validation passed:
  - `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.js` (28 tests)
  - `npm --prefix .agent test` (443 tests)

Changed files:
- `.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts`
- `.agent/src/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.ts`

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 99 | PASS | 10 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR body ties the change to #83 and linked child issues #84/#79; implementation/docs target that broader orchestration case. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | Keeps `child_issue_number` as a distinct planner field and validates explicit targets, instead of overloading free-form instructions. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Reuses existing sub-orchestrator marker parsing/formatting, child-link markers, trusted actor checks, and issue/comment helpers. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updates `.agent/docs/actions/agent-actions.md`, supported workflows, technical orchestrator docs, and `.github/prompts/agent-orchestrator.md`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Comment on accepted workflow stops | pass | 6/6 | Invalid explicit child targets stop visibly through the parent issue comment path; adoption records visible agent-authored child and parent comments. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | Missing marker comment ids throw; PR-number and closed-issue adoption targets are rejected with focused tests; terminal state updates use the trusted marker source. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | User-authored issue bodies are not trusted for child state; adopted child markers are stored in trusted agent-authored comments, and downstream child dispatch remains explicit. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | Docs describe create/reuse/adopt behavior and trusted marker ownership without adding stale file inventory tables. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Diff is limited to orchestrator handoff code, focused orchestrator tests, and matching docs/prompt text. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | partial | 6/7 | Implementation is localized and direct; the extra marker source record plumbing is somewhat heavier, but justified by body-vs-comment trust handling. |

## Notes

- The latest PR state addresses the prior warning-level follow-ups: reusable-child validation now runs after explicit reuse or adoption resolves `childIssue`, and a rerun regression verifies an existing trusted adoption marker is reused without posting a duplicate adoption comment.
- The strongest rubric alignment is the trust boundary: existing user-authored issues can be adopted without trusting user-authored markers.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No blocking or warning rubric findings. The implementation satisfies the applicable selected rubrics.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #85 lets `delegate_issue` adopt existing user-authored issues as child orchestrator targets, unblocking #83’s request to process linked issues #84 and #79 one at a time.
- The PR stores adopted child state in an agent-authored child issue comment, while keeping parent child-link and terminal report markers trusted.
- Follow-up fixes already added PR/closed-issue rejection, unconditional reusable-child validation, and an idempotency regression for reused adopted child markers.
- Docs and the orchestrator prompt were updated to describe create, reuse, and adopt behavior.

## Review
- The core adoption design is aligned with the requested workflow, but one new trust-boundary issue remains blocking.
- Reviewers disagreed on final verdict: Claude considered the PR shippable with only a pre-existing test-harness limitation, while Codex found a blocking marker-injection risk in comment-sourced child markers.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Comment-sourced child markers are too broadly trusted | BLOCKING | The new comment marker path trusts any app-authored issue comment containing `sepo-sub-orchestrator`, including model-authored comments from other routes under the same app identity. |
| Parent child-link rerun idempotency is not directly proven | WARNING | The rerun test proves no second child adoption comment is posted, but the global comment mock does not prove the parent child-link comment is also deduplicated. |
| Rubrics and docs are aligned | INFO | The rubrics review passed, and docs/prompt updates match the intended create/reuse/adopt behavior. |

## Progress
- The earlier blocking issue around adopting PR numbers or closed issues has been fixed with regressions.
- The later warning about asymmetric reusable-child validation has been fixed.
- A rerun regression now verifies already-adopted child marker comments are reused without posting another adoption marker.
- I posted a new inline comment for the remaining blocking trust-boundary issue: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/85#discussion_r3177029584

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Comment-sourced child markers are too broadly trusted</summary>

**Cause:** [`trustedSubOrchestratorMarkerFromComments`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/dc121109ff5b99fe6e81fc3c73587ff9df47a999/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L296) accepts any trusted app-authored issue comment with a `sepo-sub-orchestrator` marker. Other routes can post model-authored comments as the same app, so reserved markers can be injected through ordinary agent output.

**Candidate solutions:** Restrict comment-sourced markers to deterministic orchestrator/adoption comments with an additional route-specific marker/signature, or sanitize model-authored outputs so reserved `sepo-*` hidden markers cannot be emitted. Add regression coverage showing an unrelated app-authored answer/summary comment containing a forged marker is ignored.

**Comments:** This is the main reviewer disagreement: the adoption model is otherwise sound, but the trust predicate for comment markers is too broad.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Parent child-link rerun idempotency is not directly proven</summary>

**Cause:** The test harness returns the same `FAKE_ISSUE_COMMENTS_JSON` for every issue comment fetch, so the child and parent comment lists are not independently modeled.

**Candidate solutions:** Optionally extend the fake `gh` comment mock to vary by issue number and add a focused assertion that reruns do not post a duplicate parent child-link comment.

**Comments:** Claude rated this as a warning but also judged the production code correct by inspection; it is secondary to the blocking trust-boundary issue.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: the remaining blocking issue is a concrete trust-boundary branch change that is safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
NEEDS_REWORK

## Action Items
- [ ] Restrict trusted comment-sourced `sepo-sub-orchestrator` markers to deterministic orchestrator/adoption comments or sanitize model-authored outputs so reserved `sepo-*` markers cannot be injected.
- [ ] Add regression coverage proving unrelated app-authored model output with a forged sub-orchestrator marker is ignored.

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `fix/adopt-existing-delegate-issues`. Requested by @lolipopshock.

Addressed the latest PR #85 fix-pr request.

- Restricted comment-sourced `sepo-sub-orchestrator` state to deterministic adoption comments carrying the new `<!-- sepo-sub-orchestrator-adoption -->` marker.
- Kept issue-body markers trusted only when the issue itself is agent-authored.
- Added regressions proving forged app-authored model-output comments with `sepo-sub-orchestrator` are ignored in both explicit adoption/reuse and terminal child reporting.
- Updated existing adopted-comment fixtures to include the adoption marker.

Validation passed:
- `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.js` (30 tests)
- `npm --prefix .agent test` (445 tests)

Changed files:
- `.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts`
- `.agent/src/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.ts`

title: Allow delegate_issue to adopt existing issues
